TIMBER.name: Withered Timber
TIMBER.description: Brittle, grey wood that still holds structural integrity.

MUD.name: Damp Mud
MUD.description: Thick, viscous silt perfect for binding materials.

ROOTS.name: Fibrous Roots
ROOTS.description: Tough, stringy roots that can be used as primitive cordage.

BONE.name: Primal Bone
BONE.description: Bleached remains found deep within the silt. Exceptionally hard.

MUSHROOM.name: Fungal Cap
MUSHROOM.description: A fleshy, red cap that tastes of iron and rot.

STONE.name: Chipped Quartz
STONE.description: Hard-edged stones that can cut through tough roots.

SPORES.name: Luminous Spores
SPORES.description: Microscopic specks of biological potential. They thrum with a faint, green light.

LEAN_TO.name: Primitive Lean-to
LEAN_TO.description: A crude shelter of wood and vine. Barely keeps the resonance out.

WATTLE_HUT.name: Wattle-and-Daub Hut
WATTLE_HUT.description: A sturdy dome of woven branches plastered with thick river mud.

MOSS_CRADLE.name: Moss Cradle
MOSS_CRADLE.description: A cultivated plot of earth where the overgrowth is encouraged to reclaim the silt.
